⚖️ CLINICAL RESPONSIBILITY DISCLAIMER — K Spider AI Phase 3

"This tool is a clinical assistance system. Final responsibility lies with the prescribing doctor. K Spider AI is not responsible for misuse."

This AI tool is designed to ASSIST registered medical practitioners ONLY. All AI-generated suggestions — diagnoses, drug recommendations, interaction warnings, risk scores, and report interpretations — must be independently verified by the prescribing doctor before clinical use. This is NOT a substitute for professional medical evaluation, examination, or judgment. Patient data is stored locally only — never transmitted. By using this tool, you confirm you are a registered medical practitioner and accept full clinical responsibility for all prescriptions issued. Emergency alerts are AI-generated and require immediate clinical reassessment — they are not a replacement for clinical triage protocols.

🚀 Phase 3 Activated: PHASE 4 Disease DB 430+ conditions (10 specialties) · Emergency Alert Engine · Drug Interaction Warning System · Pregnancy/Elderly Safety Flags · Pediatric Growth Chart Calculator · Enhanced BMI/BP · Auto Risk Scoring · Multi-language Output (EN/HI/GU/MR/TA/TE/BN) · AdSense + Affiliate Earning Spaces

hCG (Beta-HCG)mIU/mL<5 (non-pregnant); varies in pregnancy
Parathyroid Hormone (PTH)pg/mL15 – 65

Important: Tumor markers are screening aids only. Elevated values must be correlated with clinical findings, imaging, and biopsy. Many non-cancer conditions also elevate these markers.
ParameterYour ValueUnitNormal RangeResult

Free PSA%>25% (lower cancer risk); <10% (high risk)

CA-125 (Ovarian / Peritoneal)U/mL<35
CA 19-9 (Pancreatic / GI)U/mL<37
AFP (Alpha-Fetoprotein)ng/mL<10 (non-pregnant adult)
CA 15-3 (Breast Cancer)U/mL<31
CA 72-4 (Gastric Cancer)U/mL<6.9
CA 50 (GI / Lung)U/mL<25
NSE (Neuron-Specific Enolase)ng/mL<17 (lung/neuroendocrine)
CYFRA 21-1 (Lung SCC)ng/mL<3.3

LDH (Lymphoma marker)U/L120 – 246
Calcitonin (Thyroid MTC)pg/mL<11.5 (M); <4.6 (F)

Values: 0 = Negative / Non-reactive, 1 = Positive / Reactive. Use numeric titers where applicable.
ParameterYour ValueUnitNormal RangeResult
HIV 1 & 2 (ELISA)0/1Non-reactive (0)

HBeAg (Hep B e-Antigen)0/1Negative (0)
Anti-HBs (Hepatitis B Antibody)mIU/mL>10 (immune); >100 (well immune)
VDRL / Syphilis (RPR)0/1Non-reactive (0)
Widal (S.typhi O — Titer)Titer<1:80 (endemic area: <1:160)
Widal (S.typhi H — Titer)Titer<1:80 (endemic area: <1:160)
Malaria (Peripheral Smear / RDT)0/1Negative (0)
Dengue NS1 Antigen0/1Negative (0)
Dengue IgM Antibody0/1Negative (0)
Dengue IgG Antibody0/1Negative (0) — past infection if pos

🫁 Pulmonary Function Test (PFT / Spirometry)
Enter actual values. % Predicted = (Actual / Predicted) × 100. Enter % predicted if available.
ParameterYour Value (%Pred)UnitNormal RangeResult
FVC (Forced Vital Capacity)% Predicted≥80% predicted
FEV1 (Forced Exp. Volume in 1 sec)% Predicted≥80% predicted
FEV1/FVC Ratio%≥70% (≥LLN) — <70% = Obstruction
FEF 25–75% (MMEF)% Predicted≥65% predicted (small airway index)
PEF (Peak Expiratory Flow)% Predicted≥80% predicted
TLC (Total Lung Capacity)% Predicted80–120% predicted
RV (Residual Volume)% Predicted80–120% predicted (>120% = air trapping)
DLCO (Diffusing Capacity CO)% Predicted≥75% predicted
DLCO/VA (KCO — Transfer Coefficient)% Predicted≥75% predicted
6-Minute Walk Test (6MWT)meters>400m (adult); predicted: 400–700m by age/gender

🌿 Allergy & IgE Panel
Total IgE is a general screening marker. Specific IgE (sIgE) tests indicate sensitization to individual allergens. Values in kUA/L: <0.35=Class 0 (none); 0.35–0.7=Class I; 0.7–3.5=Class II; 3.5–17.5=Class III; 17.5–50=Class IV; >50=Class V+
Allergen / ParameterYour ValueUnitNormal / ClassResult
Total IgEIU/mL<100 (adult normal); <150 (atopic tendency)
Specific IgE: Dust Mite (D.pteronyssinus)kUA/L<0.35 (Class 0 = No allergy)
Specific IgE: House DustkUA/L<0.35
Specific IgE: Cat DanderkUA/L<0.35
Specific IgE: Dog DanderkUA/L<0.35
Specific IgE: CockroachkUA/L<0.35
Specific IgE: Pollen Mix (Grass)kUA/L<0.35
Specific IgE: Mould (Alternaria)kUA/L<0.35
Specific IgE: Cow's MilkkUA/L<0.35
Specific IgE: Egg WhitekUA/L<0.35
Specific IgE: WheatkUA/L<0.35
Specific IgE: PeanutkUA/L<0.35
Specific IgE: SoybeankUA/L<0.35
Specific IgE: Shrimp/ShellfishkUA/L<0.35
Specific IgE: LatexkUA/L<0.35
Eosinophil Count (Absolute)cells/μL<500 (mild if 500–1500; severe >1500)
